constraints: The first valid local-instance clear marks the room globally complete and unlocks coarse shared doors map access and teleports
Grant the room's one-time major reward to every eligible connected player including life-exhausted spectators without duplicate granting
Players already fighting in another local instance may finish for remaining personal ordinary loot or withdraw
Personal enemies pickups unsecured loot deaths lives banking and immediate feedback resolve locally
A local death or withdrawal cannot relock globally completed progress
Prevent farming duplicate major rewards and permanent durable-state disagreement
Allow future deep shared-objective rooms to use stricter synchronization separately
After the first valid global clear non-participants cannot start a new combat instance in that room during the same run
Players already inside an active instance may still finish or withdraw under D-195
Non-participants may miss personal XP because the globally cleared room does not replay
Qualifying non-XP events such as strongbox drops create matching personal reward events for other eligible players
Mirrored rewards remain personally owned and follow unsecured-loot death-loss banking deterministic grant and save rules
Respawn teleport reconnect and later entry preserve global room completion
Each eligible player may receive the mirrored reward event at most once
Each eligible player independently rolls the complete reward including strongbox tier and eventual contents
Use shared mission difficulty and challenge settings plus only the reward owner's account-level eligibility
Never let teammate level gear performance progression or reward result alter another player's odds
Derive a unique deterministic personal seed from the shared event and stable player identity
Allow players to receive different tiers and items from the same event
Keep every result personal and subject to owner-specific pickup banking death-loss and persistence rules
Create each eligible player's personal physical reward at the original event location
Require the owning player to reach and collect the drop
Do not automatically secure or permanently grant the reward merely because the player was eligible
Do not allow other players to steal consume or redirect an owned personal drop
Preserve normal unsecured-loot and banking risk after collection
A future forced-rendezvous system may relocate or carry all uncollected boxes belonging to a teleported player
Defer exact spectator geometry and relocated-drop presentation rules to co-op implementation
Allow ready players to initiate a visible regroup vote or countdown at authored major transitions
Warn separated players and give them a short collection or preparation window
Do not permit unrestricted forced teleporting as a combat escape
Transfer or safely relocate the teleported player's remaining owned strongboxes
Prevent one idle or malicious player from blocking progression indefinitely
Prevent one impatient player from arbitrarily teleporting the party
Treat vote threshold countdown combat cancellation leader privileges and disconnect handling as playtest variables
Reserve an eligible disconnected player's uncollected physical drops for a bounded reconnect grace period
Do not allow other players to steal or claim reserved drops
Restore normal physical pickup responsibility when the owner reconnects within the grace period
Move reserved drops through the protected destination flow if forced rendezvous occurs during disconnection
Allow explicit session departure or grace-period expiry to forfeit the uncollected reward under the final policy
Use stable event and owner identifiers plus atomic transitions to prevent duplicate grants
Seriously test timeout boundaries repeated reconnects crashes host migration death spectator state rendezvous save interruption packet loss and intentional disconnect abuse
Leave exact grace-period duration to prototypes and realistic network testing

open_questions_added: Exact reconnect grace-period duration and forfeiture messaging
Spectator pickup or protected-transfer behavior for physical personal rewards
Vote threshold and countdown tuning for forced rendezvous
Host-migration behavior while rewards are reserved or in destination transfer
Unity relay and networking package selection
Exact client trust validation and anti-cheat boundary
Cross-region latency jitter and packet-loss acceptance thresholds
Future shared-objective synchronization beyond the first co-op MVP

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting what the one-level internal vertical slice must prove before broader content production begins: basic feature completeness; exceptional core movement and combat feel plus repeat-run desire even with temporary content; or a staged hybrid gate requiring both a fun repeated-play benchmark and one thin end-to-end production loop. Preserve the one-level automated weapons factory slice, six to eight base weapons, four or five ordinary enemy roles plus an upgraded droid climax, minimal real strongbox and randomized shop loop, Unity C# architecture, Windows-first offline play, deterministic replay and difficulty direction, solo-led AI-assisted production, and the rule that exact coefficients remain playtest variables. Include kill criteria, replay sessions, external testers, content polish, placeholder tolerance, performance stability, save reliability, instrumentation, scope expansion risk, and what may remain deferred. Put the recommendation after all options at the end of the card. Begin a new persistence batch D-201 through D-210 unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
